tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated serum creatinine and chloride, suggesting possible renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising suspicion for renal tubular dysfunction or urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical findings.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
